text.title: llamaindex-sql-query Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 high-impact failure modes for llamaindex-sql-query with detectors and fixes that map back to rules in 01-core-rules.xml.

antipattern.symptom: Agent drops a customer table during dev
antipattern.fix: Enforce read-only at the DSN; CI lint refuses write-capable users.
antipattern.detector: Try a DROP via NL query; should fail with permission denied, not via 'agent refused'.

antipattern.symptom: Cost blows up on first query against a 200-table DB
antipattern.fix: Use ObjectIndex over SQLTableSchema; retrieve top-3 relevant tables per query.
antipattern.detector: Token count per query; should drop 5-10x.

antipattern.symptom: Generated SQL has a fabricated column
antipattern.root-cause: LLM hallucinated under pressure; no validation.
antipattern.fix: Run SQLDatabase.get_table_columns and validate generated SQL columns against schema; reject if mismatch.
antipattern.detector: Run a known-bad query; verify rejection.

antipattern.fix: Force LIMIT clause; cap max_returned_rows in synth.
antipattern.detector: Stress test with broad query; verify LIMIT presence.
